text: AI agents are getting faster at changing code. But can “done” actually pass human review?
Halba turns reports, source files, diffs, and machine receipts into one bounded proof packet.
GPT-5.6 extracts atomic claims and citations. Deterministic guards check the actual bytes.
Open the real patch, exact line range, hash, reasoning boundary, and guard result together.
Unsupported, stale, contradictory, and uncertain claims become focused human review gates.
The public corpus passes nine of nine cases with exact source-grounding precision and recall.
Halba: agent claims in, traceable proof out—and a clear boundary for human judgment.
